name: BRICS Framework 2009 (Brazil + Russia + India + China + South Africa + Expansion States)
summary: The BRICS Framework is a global supranational meta-framework non-treaty informal-cooperation framework adopted by the 5 founding emerging-economy states in Yekaterinburg — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Global-South-emerging-economies non-treaty supranational framework operating on formal non-treaty informal-cooperation framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal China-Russia-India-Brazil-South-Africa Global-South-co-anchor framework (BRICS Framework first BRIC Summit 16 June 2009 in Yekaterinburg Russia by Brazil + Russia + India + China + South Africa joined 13 April 2011 at 3rd BRICS Summit Sanya China + BRICS Expansion BRICS+ effective 1 January 2024 with Egypt + Ethiopia + Iran + UAE + Indonesia 6 January 2025 — formal foundational BRICS framework establishing comprehensive BRICS organisation framework operating non-treaty informal-cooperation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-supranational-frameworks which operate as formal binding-treaty frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Global-South-emerging-economies non-treaty supranational framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal non-treaty informal-cooperation framework + ~46% global population framework + ~35% global GDP PPP framework + ~50% global oil production framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Global-South-de-dollarisation framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal G7-counterbalance framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal BRICS New Development Bank NDB framework (BRICS New Development Bank NDB established 15 July 2014 at 6th BRICS Summit Fortaleza Brazil + effective 7 July 2015 + headquartered in Shanghai China providing formal BRICS-led development-bank framework — formal foundational BRICS development-bank framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational framework operating formal BRICS-led development-bank framework + only modern Global-South-led multilateral development-bank framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA framework (BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA Treaty signed 15 July 2014 at 6th BRICS Summit Fortaleza Brazil + effective 30 July 2015 providing formal USD 100 billion BRICS-led reserve-arrangement framework — formal foundational BRICS reserve-arrangement framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational framework operating formal BRICS-led IMF-counterbalance framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal BRICS Expansion BRICS+ 2024-2025 framework (BRICS Expansion BRICS+ adopted 24 August 2023 at 15th BRICS Summit Johannesburg South Africa + effective 1 January 2024 with Egypt + Ethiopia + Iran + Saudi Arabia + UAE + Argentina-withdrew framework + Indonesia joined 6 January 2025 — formal foundational BRICS Expansion framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational framework operating formal Expansion framework with operational expansion framework via BRICS+ framework). The BRICS Framework is operating upon 10 BRICS member states + ~9 BRICS partner states as of 2026 (Brazil + China + Egypt + Ethiopia + India + Indonesia + Iran + Russia + South Africa + UAE) — comprehensive Global-South-emerging-economies coverage framework. The Framework is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal BRICS framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Global-South cross-border-children's-rights framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework. The BRICS Annual Summit + BRICS Chairmanship + BRICS New Development Bank NDB + BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA + BRICS Business Council + BRICS Civil Forum are the formal BRICS bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The BRICS Framework is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: First BRIC Summit Declaration
statutory_framework.relevance: First BRIC Summit Declaration adopted 16 June 2009 at first BRIC Summit Yekaterinburg Russia — formal foundational BRIC framework adopted establishing comprehensive BRIC organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: BRICS Expansion 2010
statutory_framework.relevance: BRICS Expansion South Africa joined 23 December 2010 + formally invited 13 April 2011 at 3rd BRICS Summit Sanya China — formal BRIC-to-BRICS expansion framework.

statutory_framework.citation: BRICS New Development Bank NDB Articles of Agreement 2014
statutory_framework.title: BRICS NDB Articles of Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: BRICS New Development Bank NDB Articles of Agreement signed 15 July 2014 at 6th BRICS Summit Fortaleza Brazil + effective 7 July 2015 — formal foundational BRICS development-bank framework.

statutory_framework.citation: BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA Treaty 2014
statutory_framework.title: BRICS CRA Treaty
statutory_framework.relevance: BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA Treaty signed 15 July 2014 at 6th BRICS Summit Fortaleza Brazil + effective 30 July 2015 — formal foundational BRICS reserve-arrangement framework.

statutory_framework.title: BRICS Expansion BRICS+
statutory_framework.relevance: BRICS Expansion BRICS+ adopted 24 August 2023 at 15th BRICS Summit Johannesburg South Africa + effective 1 January 2024 with Egypt + Ethiopia + Iran + Saudi Arabia + UAE + Indonesia 6 January 2025 — formal foundational BRICS Expansion framework.

apex_courts.name: BRICS Annual Summit
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for BRICS.

apex_courts.name: BRICS Chairmanship
apex_courts.role: Formal annual presidency body for BRICS.

apex_courts.name: BRICS Business Council
apex_courts.role: Formal BRICS business engagement framework body.

professional_regulators.name: BRICS Chairmanship Sherpa
professional_regulators.role: BRICS Framework implementation framework — rotates annually with BRICS Chairmanship.

anonymisation_convention: BRICS-related communications operate via BRICS Annual Summit + BRICS Chairmanship framework; individual communications not formally accepted under BRICS framework.

key_developments.title: BRIC acronym coined
key_developments.description: BRIC acronym coined 2001 by Jim O'Neill Goldman Sachs Chief Economist in 'Building Better Global Economic BRICs' Global Economics Paper 66 — predecessor framework to BRICS.

key_developments.title: First BRIC Summit
key_developments.description: First BRIC Summit held 16 June 2009 in Yekaterinburg Russia by Brazil + Russia + India + China — formal foundational BRIC framework operational.

key_developments.title: South Africa joins BRICS
key_developments.description: South Africa joined BRICS 23 December 2010 + formally invited 13 April 2011 at 3rd BRICS Summit Sanya China — formal BRIC-to-BRICS expansion framework.

key_developments.title: BRICS NDB + CRA established
key_developments.description: BRICS New Development Bank NDB + BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA Treaty signed 15 July 2014 at 6th BRICS Summit Fortaleza Brazil — formal foundational BRICS development-bank + reserve-arrangement frameworks adopted.

key_developments.title: BRICS NDB + CRA enters force
key_developments.description: BRICS NDB entered force 7 July 2015 + BRICS CRA entered force 30 July 2015 — formal foundational BRICS development-bank + reserve-arrangement frameworks operational.

key_developments.title: BRICS Expansion BRICS+ adopted
key_developments.description: BRICS Expansion BRICS+ adopted 24 August 2023 at 15th BRICS Summit Johannesburg South Africa inviting Argentina + Egypt + Ethiopia + Iran + Saudi Arabia + UAE — formal foundational BRICS Expansion framework adopted.

key_developments.title: BRICS+ enters force + Argentina withdraws
key_developments.description: BRICS+ effective 1 January 2024 with Egypt + Ethiopia + Iran + UAE + Argentina-withdrew framework + Saudi Arabia-uncertain framework — formal BRICS Expansion framework operational.

key_developments.title: Indonesia joins BRICS + BRICS Partner States framework
key_developments.description: Indonesia joined BRICS 6 January 2025 + BRICS Partner States framework adopted 2025 with Belarus + Bolivia + Cuba + Kazakhstan + Malaysia + Nigeria + Thailand + Uganda + Uzbekistan partner-state framework — formal BRICS expansion + partner-state framework operational.

structural_findings: BRICS Framework operates a supranational non-treaty informal-cooperation framework — places BRICS Framework in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern Global-South-emerging-economies non-treaty supranational framework operating on formal non-treaty informal-cooperation framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal China-Russia-India-Brazil-South-Africa Global-South-co-anchor framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Global-South-emerging-economies non-treaty supranational framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal non-treaty informal-cooperation framework + ~46% global population framework + ~35% global GDP PPP framework + ~50% global oil production framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Global-South-de-dollarisation framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal G7-counterbalance framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal BRICS New Development Bank NDB framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational framework operating formal BRICS-led development-bank framework + only modern Global-South-led multilateral development-bank framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement CRA framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational framework operating formal BRICS-led IMF-counterbalance framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal BRICS Expansion BRICS+ 2024-2025 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational framework operating formal Expansion framework with operational expansion framework via BRICS+ framework.
10 BRICS member state + ~9 BRICS partner state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive Global-South-emerging-economies coverage framework.

2001 BRIC acronym + 2009 first BRIC Summit + 2010 South Africa joins + 2014 NDB + CRA + 2015 entry into force + 2023 BRICS+ adopted + 2024 BRICS+ effective + Argentina withdraws + 2025 Indonesia joins + Partner States framework BRICS-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

BRICS Framework + NDB + CRA + BRICS Expansion BRICS+ + BRICS Business Council + BRICS Civil Forum formal sextet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via Global-South-non-treaty-informal-cooperation framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating non-treaty informal-cooperation framework relative to other regional + global frameworks.
editorial_notes: BRICS Framework jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global Global-South-emerging-economies non-treaty informal-cooperation meta-framework (BRICS Framework 2009 + BRIC acronym 2001 predecessor + South Africa 2010 + NDB + CRA 2014/2015 + BRICS+ 2023/2024 + Indonesia 2025 + Argentina withdrew + Partner States 2025). Only modern Global-South-emerging-economies non-treaty supranational framework globally + only modern supranational framework operating formal non-treaty informal-cooperation framework + ~46% global population + ~35% global GDP PPP + ~50% global oil production + only modern supranational framework operating formal Global-South-de-dollarisation + G7-counterbalance framework + central jurisdiction of formal BRICS NDB Shanghai + central jurisdiction of formal BRICS CRA USD 100 billion + central jurisdiction of formal BRICS+ Expansion framework + 10 BRICS member states (Brazil + China + Egypt + Ethiopia + India + Indonesia + Iran + Russia + South Africa + UAE) + ~9 partner states + 2001 BRIC acronym + 2009 first Summit + 2010 South Africa + 2014 NDB + CRA + 2015 entry into force + 2023 BRICS+ adopted + 2024 effective + Argentina withdraws + 2025 Indonesia + Partner States framework BRICS-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: BRICS framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Global-South cross-border-children's-rights framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating non-treaty informal-cooperation framework relative to other regional + global frameworks.

Joins global + supranational + Global-South-emerging-economies-non-treaty-informal-cooperation cluster + BRICS-Framework-only-Global-South-emerging-economies-non-treaty-supranational-framework-globally-distinctive + non-treaty-informal-cooperation + China-Russia-India-Brazil-South-Africa-Global-South-co-anchor + 46-percent-global-population + 35-percent-global-GDP-PPP + 50-percent-global-oil-production + Global-South-de-dollarisation + G7-counterbalance + BRICS-NDB-Shanghai-development-bank + BRICS-CRA-USD-100-billion-IMF-counterbalance + BRICS+-Expansion-2024-Egypt-Ethiopia-Iran-UAE + Argentina-withdrew-2024 + Indonesia-joined-2025 + Partner-States-framework-2025 + 10-BRICS-member-states + 9-BRICS-partner-states + 2001-BRIC-acronym-Jim-O'Neill-Goldman-Sachs + 2009-Yekaterinburg-first-Summit + 2010-South-Africa-BRIC-to-BRICS + 2014-Fortaleza-NDB-CRA + limited-PA-intersection-via-Global-South-non-treaty-framework clusters within the corpus.
